description: Vistra is a U.S. integrated electricity company combining large-scale power generation, retail electricity brands, and a growing zero-carbon generation portfolio across competitive power markets.

overview.title: Business Profile
paragraphs: Vistra operates as an integrated power company, pairing electricity generation assets with retail electricity offerings. Its business is concentrated in competitive U.S. power markets, where reliability, fuel mix, capacity pricing, and customer acquisition all matter more than utility-sector slogans usually admit.

The company’s retail side sells electricity to residential, commercial, and industrial customers, while its generation fleet supplies power from a mix of natural gas, coal, nuclear, solar, and battery storage assets.

overview.title: Market Role
paragraphs: Vistra is not a traditional regulated local utility with a captive service territory. It is closer to a merchant power and retail energy platform, exposed to wholesale power prices, generation economics, customer churn, and regional grid rules.

That structure makes the company useful for the registry because it sits at the intersection of consumer energy choice, centralized generation, decarbonization claims, and the practical constraints of keeping the lights on.

overview.title: Products And Services
paragraphs: The company’s core product categories are retail electricity plans and power generation. Retail offerings are delivered through brands including TXU Energy and other regional energy suppliers, depending on market and customer segment.
On the generation side, Vistra owns and operates power plants and energy storage assets that sell electricity, capacity, and related grid services into wholesale markets.

overview.title: Registry Relevance
paragraphs: Vistra should be evaluated against alternatives in distributed energy, community solar, municipal and cooperative power, demand response, home energy management, open grid data, and customer-owned generation.

The Free The World angle is not simply whether Vistra produces cleaner electricity, but whether customers and communities get meaningful control, transparency, portability, and resilience instead of another polished bill from a very large power seller.

sourceSuggestions.title: Vistra Corporate Website
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary source for company positioning, business segments, generation portfolio, retail brands, and sustainability messaging.

sourceSuggestions.title: Vistra Investor Relations
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary source for investor presentations, SEC filings, earnings materials, financial structure, and management commentary.

sourceSuggestions.title: Vistra Form 10-K

sourceSuggestions.title: TXU Energy
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Key retail electricity brand showing customer-facing plans, pricing posture, service model, and product claims.

sourceSuggestions.title: Vistra Sustainability
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Source for emissions goals, generation transition claims, community impact framing, and environmental disclosures.

sourceSuggestions.title: U.S. Energy Information Administration Electricity Data
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Independent public data source for U.S. electricity generation, fuel mix, power markets, and sector-level context.

sourceSuggestions.title: CompaniesMarketCap Vistra Market Cap
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Useful for registry ranking context and market-cap snapshot validation.
